segments.text: MHC Gene organization

Major Histocompatibility Complex (MHC).
A complex of genes encoding cell surface molecules that are required for antigen presentation to T cells and for rapid graft rejections.  It is called the H-2 complex in the mouse and the HLA complex in humans.

HLA
Human Leukocyte Antigen system
H-2 for mice, and HLA for humans is commonly referred to
centromere           telomere
MHC genes are highly polymorphic

Locus			   Number of Alleles
HLA-A				2013
HLA-B				2605
HLA-C				1551
HLA-E, F, G			few
While the absolute number of alleles is not really settled on yet, there is a very high degree of polymorphism at the HLA locus
Kuby: L321 Textbook

Any one individual expresses only a small number of MHC molecules, up to six different ( so is “polygenic”) class I, and 12 or more different class II molecules.
This limited number of MHC molecule presents an enormous array of different antigenic peptides to T-cells.
A given MHC molecule can bind numerous different peptides.
Co-dominant expression of Mouse MHC I and II genes

Haplotype
In  MHC genes, it is the set of alleles of linked genes present on one parental chromosome
The MHC loci were first discovered as genes that were involved in histological compatibility between donors and recipients

Universal Donor:
O
Universal receipient”
AB

Note: Should associate
MHC Class I with CD8 and TC cells
MHC Class II with CD4 and TH cells

Class III MHC genes
Encode secreted proteins that have immune functions, including components of the complement system and molecules involved in inflammation

Non-Peptide Antigens
recognized by natural killer T cells

Major Terms and Concepts
MHC
H2
HLA
Class I, II, and III MHC genes
Haplotype
Polygenic
Highly polymorphic
Professional Antigen Presenting Cells
MHC Class I with CD8 and TC cells
MHC Class II with CD4 and TH cells
Mate choice in humans

Class I MHC genes
Encode glycoproteins expressed on the surface of nearly all nucleated cells; the major function of the class I gene products is presentation of peptide antigens to TC cells

Class II MHC genes
Encode glycoprotiens expressed primarily on antigen-presenting cells (macrophages, dendritic cells, and B cells), where they present processed antigenic peptides to TH cells
